examples.title: Algorithm: Parallel Data Decomposition
examples.description: Demonstrates how to decompose a problem into parallel tasks using domain decomposition
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ParallelArraySum
INPUT: Array A[0..n-1], Number of processors P
OUTPUT: Sum of all elements in Array A

BEGIN
 // Step 1: Partition the data
 chunk_size = n / P
 FOR each processor i from 0 to P-1 DO
 start_index = i * chunk_size
 end_index = start_index + chunk_size - 1
 IF i = P-1 THEN
 end_index = n - 1 // Last processor gets remaining elements
 END IF
 ASSIGN A[start_index..end_index] TO processor i
 END FOR

 // Step 2: Each processor computes local sum in parallel
 PARALLEL FOR each processor i from 0 to P-1 DO
 local_sum[i] = ComputePartialSum(A, start_index, end_index)
 END PARALLEL FOR

 // Step 3: Synchronize at barrier
 BARRIER

 // Step 4: Reduce local sums to global sum (tree reduction)
 WHILE P > 1 DO
 FOR i from 0 to (P/2)-1 DO
 local_sum[i] = local_sum[i] + local_sum[i + P/2]
 processor i continues
 processor (i + P/2) becomes idle
 END FOR
 P = P / 2
 BARRIER
 END WHILE

 global_sum = local_sum[0]
 RETURN global_sum
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates domain decomposition where an array is partitioned among processors. Each processor computes a partial sum locally, then all processors synchronize at a barrier before combining results using tree-based reduction for efficiency.

examples.title: Python: Parallel Array Processing with Multiprocessing
examples.description: Demonstrates parallel data processing using Python's multiprocessing module
examples.code:
import multiprocessing as mp
import numpy as np
import time

def partial_sum(start_idx, end_idx, data, result_queue, proc_id):
 """Each process computes sum of its assigned portion of the array."""
 partial = np.sum(data[start_idx:end_idx])
 result_queue.put((proc_id, partial))
 print(f"Process {proc_id}: Sum of elements [{start_idx}:{end_idx}] = {partial}")

def parallel_array_sum(data, num_processes):
 """Main function to compute array sum in parallel."""
 n = len(data)
 chunk_size = n // num_processes
 result_queue = mp.Queue()
 processes = []

 # Step 1: Create processes and partition data
 for i in range(num_processes):
 start_idx = i * chunk_size
 # Last process gets remaining elements
 end_idx = start_idx + chunk_size if i < num_processes - 1 else n
 
 p = mp.Process(
 target=partial_sum,
 args=(start_idx, end_idx, data, result_queue, i)
 )
 processes.append(p)

 # Step 2: Start all processes (parallel execution begins)
 start_time = time.time()
 for p in processes:
 p.start()

 # Step 3: Wait for all processes to complete
 for p in processes:
 p.join()

 # Step 4: Collect and combine results
 results = []
 while not result_queue.empty():
 results.append(result_queue.get())
 
 total_sum = sum(r[1] for r in results)
 elapsed = time.time() - start_time

 return total_sum, elapsed

# Example usage and comparison
if __name__ == "__main__":
 # Create test data
 n = 10_000_000
 data = np.random.rand(n)

 # Sequential calculation for verification
 start = time.time()
 sequential_sum = np.sum(data)
 sequential_time = time.time() - start
 print(f"Sequential sum: {sequential_sum:.2f}, Time: {sequential_time:.4f}s")

 # Parallel calculation with different process counts
 for num_procs in [2, 4, 8]:
 parallel_sum, parallel_time = parallel_array_sum(data, num_procs)
 print(f"\n{num_procs} processes: Sum={parallel_sum:.2f}, Time={parallel_time:.4f}s")
 print(f"Speedup: {sequential_time/parallel_time:.2f}x")

# Example Output:
# Sequential sum: 5000034.56, Time: 0.0312s
# Process 0: Sum of elements [0:2500000] = 1250012.34
# Process 1: Sum of elements [2500000:5000000] = 1249987.89
# Process 2: Sum of elements [5000000:7500000] = 1250021.45
# Process 3: Sum of elements [7500000:10000000] = 1250012.88
# 4 processes: Sum=5000034.56, Time=0.0891s
# Speedup: 0.35x
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates parallel data decomposition using the multiprocessing module. Each process gets a chunk of the array, computes a local sum, and the main process combines results. Note that for small computations, the overhead of process creation can exceed the parallel speedup, demonstrating why parallel programming requires careful consideration of overhead vs. benefit.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n/p) for each process, O(n) total
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for data + O(p) for result storage

examples.title: C: OpenMP Parallel For Loop
examples.description: Demonstrates parallel execution using OpenMP pragmas for shared-memory programming
examples.explanation: This C program with OpenMP demonstrates shared-memory parallelism. The '#pragma omp parallel for' directive automatically distributes loop iterations across available threads. The 'reduction(+:par_sum)' clause ensures each thread has a private copy that is combined at the end, avoiding race conditions. The program shows the speedup achieved compared to sequential execution.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n/p) where p is number of threads
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for array + O(p) stack space

examples.title: C: MPI Distributed Memory Programming
examples.description: Demonstrates message passing between processes for distributed-memory parallelism
examples.explanation: This MPI program demonstrates distributed-memory parallelism where each process has its own private memory space. MPI_Scatter distributes chunks of the array to each process, they compute local sums in parallel, then MPI_Reduce combines all results at the root process. This follows the MIMD architecture where each processor executes independent instructions on its local data.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n/p) computation + O(n) communication for scatter/reduce
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n/p) per process + O(n) at root for original array
